tax: Placed at genus rank, Ignis is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a bipedal vent-pressure combustion body distinguishable from the Flameling and Cinderling by persistent humanoid form and char-ring footprint. A new species belongs here only if it shows a bipedal combustion body with its own vent-pressure formation mechanism, combustion colour, and char-ring field sign.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a flame humanoid ranging 1.2 m (minor, blue-white) to 6 m (major, deep red-orange). A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — combustion colour (blue-white for minor, orange-yellow standard, deep red-orange major, three-layer for nobilis), char-ring size and depth, and ceiling-blackening pattern — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Ignis occupies vent passages, vent jets, vent convergences, and primary vent zones throughout the lower tiers. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where vent-fault pressure — narrow, single, convergent, or primary — ignites a combustion form into a bipedal shape and recur with the vent re-pressurises and the combustion form re-ignites, sustaining themselves on vent-fault fuel at various pressures.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: all Ignis forms are territorial, hold a passage or vent zone, and sense approach by radiant heat; they differ in combustion colour, char-ring pattern, movement speed, and threat response (advance vs dart vs fill-passage vs inside-out display). Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a bipedal flame body, combustion-colour field sign, char-ring footprint, vent dependence, and a smothering/flooding/fuel-starvation failure mode specific to each form. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: ignition, passage-filling, fuel-draw repair, and low-flame disguise; the nobilis form adds fuel-independence, inside-out threat display, and Ignis-detection sensing. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Ignis matters because the Ignis genus accounts for most fatal vent-passage fires in the lower gorge and is the primary subject of Forgemaster vent-clearance protocols. It lets survey reports sort humanoid flame hazards by combustion colour and char-ring pattern, enabling a crew to identify which form is active and plan the correct suppression approach without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Ignis is the genus-rank grouping of roughly humanoid flame-body elementals of the vent-pressure kind within the True Elementals. Its members share a bipedal combustion body held by vent-fault pressure, with a bright torso-core and char-ring footprint distinguishing them from ordinary gas-flames, and divide below into species groups by the vent pressure type — narrow high-pressure (minor), standard vent (standard), convergent multi-vent (major), or deep primary differentiated (nobilis). The Ignis genus is the journal's formal name for what grey-folk call 'the fire that walks like a person' — the classic bipedal fire elemental, recognised across all four known forms by char-ring pattern and combustion colour.
